title: Reply with exactly: explorer OK
summary_text: Concise audit findings (read-only): 1. **Staleness logic is split and inconsistent** - `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh` and `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-claim.sh` each decide claim staleness differently, mainly around `updated_at_epoch` + PID checks, so cleanup behavior can diverge. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) 2. **Claim reclamation can be too aggressive with weak liveness proof** - Reconciliation currently relies on PID freshness and queue state first, then releases after TTL; compact/session state isn’t required to be checked before release/kill intent, so false-positive stale detection is possible (e.g., orphaned PID/missing pid while work is still active elsewhere). - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) 3. **“Stop file” path is one-way and often ineffective** - `workspace_stop_file_for_claim()` writes a stop marker under workspace, but the driver does not consume it as an actionable stop signal; it does not ensure graceful handoff/termination of active runtime work. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) - [kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh) 4. **No automatic stale compact compaction/removal path** - Reconciler has no compact-lifecycle branch; stale/abandoned compact artifacts are only surfaced for visibility, not reclaimed. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) - [kernel-compact-artifact.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh) 5. **Recovery does not enforce cross-component coherence** - Recovery can rehydrate run state without verifying claim/compact staleness alignment first, so stale claims and stale compacts can be independently reactivated or left inconsistent. - [kernel-run-recovery.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh) 6. **Doctor observability only warns, doesn’t operationalize cleanup safely** - `codex-kernel-guard.sh` classifies stale items but does not provide a deterministic reconciliation/remediation action contract tied to exact stale evidence. - [codex-kernel-guard.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh) Smallest safe patch set: 1. **Add one canonical stale predicate in reconciler and gate all reclaim paths through it** - In `kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh`, introduce `is_definitely_stale_claim(claim)` with strict criteria: - `state` is non-terminal - `updated_at_epoch + ttl < now` - no valid live run context from **any** of: PID, tmux session, compact heartbeat/metadata - optional queue mismatch checks preserved as secondary signals - This avoids active-run false positives and unifies behavior. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) 2. **Guard reclaim action with explicit “safe-to-clean” proof** - Only transition to `awaiting_human` / release when canonical predicate returns true. - Add a short grace window before hard release to reduce false positives on transient PID gaps. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh) 3. **Compact cleanup as explicit optional mode (dry-run default)** - Add reconcile mode `--cleanup-compacts[=true|false]` (off by default). - Candidate compact deletion only when: - associated claim is terminal/irrecoverable - compact is stale by modified time or explicit policy - no active tmux/compact heartbeat - Keep non-terminal claims immune regardless of age. - [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtim...
